name: Dolega, Panama

visa.notes: Same Pensionado program as all Panama. $1,000/mo pension income required. Dolega is a quiet alternative to the more expat-saturated Boquete — 25 km north — with meaningfully lower rents.

rent.notes: No rental tax in Panama. Dolega rents run 30-50% below Boquete. Median ~$525/mo for a modest 2-3BR home per 2025 listings (Encuentra24, Rent By Owner). Furnished homes with expat amenities trend toward the upper end. David 15-20 min south for shopping, hospital, airport.

groceries.notes: 2 adults + pet food; shop primarily in David (Super Baru, PriceSmart, Romero). Local Dolega market for produce. Prices similar to Boquete — same regional supply chain.

electricity.notes: EDECHI. Dolega at ~1,150 ft elevation — warmer than Boquete, cooler than David. Fans sufficient most mornings and evenings; AC useful on hot afternoons (Mar-Apr peak). Higher than Boquete, much lower than David.

gas.notes: Propane (tanque) for cooking. No natural gas grid.

water.notes: IDAAN or community water cooperative. Mountain water affordable.

sewage.notes: Septic systems common in rural Dolega properties.

trash.notes: Garbage collection typically included in rent or covered by municipality.

utilities.taxes: ITBMS 7% on electricity. Water exempt for residential.
utilities.notes: Electricity, propane, water, sewage. Internet is in entertainment. Trash typically included in rent.

healthcare.notes: Private insurance required (~$175-250/person/mo). No public hospital in Dolega — Hospital Chiriquí and Hospital Mae Lewis both in David (15-20 min drive). Routine care at local Dolega clinics and pharmacies. Anti-VEGF and ophthalmology in David or Panama City. Pensionado discount 15-25% on services. Pre-existing condition exclusions common first 12-24 months.

petCare.notes: Basic vet care available in David (15-20 min). Fewer pet services than Boquete. Local vets affordable but limited specialty capability.

petDaycare.notes: No commercial pet daycare in Dolega. Informal pet sitters via expat/local network. David has a few boarding options. Expect to pay $12-18/visit for in-home sitting.

petGrooming.notes: Groomers available in David; may need to travel. Fewer options than Boquete.

transportation.notes: Car strongly recommended — no expat-targeted local transit. David runs ($1-2 bus or $5-8 taxi). Gas, insurance, and David supply runs (~2-3x/week for many expats). Closer to David than Boquete, which reduces per-trip cost slightly.

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+internet (slower than Boquete — 50-150Mbps), Netflix, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+, NFL RedZone (DAZN). Fewer dining-out options than Boquete; David restaurants 15-20 min away. Small-town social scene; Boquete expat events accessible.

phoneCell.notes: Google Fi Flexible (US number) + +Movil or Claro prepaid (local). Same setup as Boquete region. 2 phones.

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Private system OOP: specialist visits $40-80 in David, anti-VEGF injections $400-800 each at David/Panama City clinics. Labs $15-30/panel. Pensionado discounts offset some costs. Serious specialties may require travel to Panama City.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household. Farmacia Arrocha and local pharmacies in David carry most common medications at Panama prices.

taxes.notes: Panama territorial tax system: foreign-sourced income (SS, pension, foreign investments) not taxed. No state income tax.

stateIncomeTax.label: Panama Income Tax
stateIncomeTax.exemptions: Territorial system: foreign-sourced income NOT taxed in Panama.

taxes.notes: Territorial tax: only Panama-sourced income taxed. Foreign pension/SS/investment income is fully exempt.

healthcare.system: Private (Hospital Chiriquí and Mae Lewis in David, 15-20 min)
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: varies by plan; good pharmacy availability in David

lifestyle.internetSpeed: 50-150Mbps; fiber expanding but patchier than Boquete
lifestyle.notes: Quiet agricultural town. Tight-knit community with outdoor markets, local fiestas, and a slower pace than Boquete. Potrerillos Arriba nearby offers scenic highland views. Smaller expat community than Boquete — more immersive local living.

pros: No income tax on foreign income
Cool highland climate (no AC most of the year)
15-20 min from David hospital, airport, and full shopping
25 min from Boquete for expat social events and restaurants

cons.text: Smaller expat community than Boquete — fewer English-speaking neighbors
Fewer dining, entertainment, and pet services than Boquete
Car is not optional — no local transit
No hospital in town — David for all medical needs
Safety & crime: see cited reports for current figures by city and country
